seoTitle: Color Code Dropdown Options in Smartsheet Easily
metaDescription: Use Smartsheet's Conditional Formatting engine to automatically color-code dropdown values, making status changes visible at a glance.
intro: A dropdown column full of plain text is easy to overlook. Smartsheet's Conditional Formatting engine lets you map specific colors to specific dropdown values, so status shifts become instantly scannable. This guide covers building those rules and stacking them cleanly.

steps.heading: Find your dropdown column
steps.body: Open your tracking sheet and locate the dropdown column you want to style.

steps.heading: Open Conditional Formatting
steps.body: Access the Conditional Formatting rules panel from the main toolbar menu.

steps.heading: Define your criteria
steps.body: Set precise logical rules targeting specific single-select or multi-select dropdown values.

steps.heading: Customize the visual style
steps.body: Assign row-level color bars, font styles, or background fills for each rule you create.

steps.heading: Stack multiple rules carefully
steps.body: Build a full color scale like red, yellow, green for priority or risk, making sure the rules don't conflict with existing formatting.

faq.question: Can Smartsheet automatically color a row based on a dropdown value?
faq.answer: Yes, Conditional Formatting rules can apply row-level colors, font changes, or fills based on the dropdown selection.

faq.question: Does color coding work in Gantt and Card views too?
faq.answer: Yes, once set up, the color coding renders natively across Grid, Gantt, and Card views.

faq.question: How many formatting rules can I stack?
faq.answer: You can add multiple rules sequentially, though it's worth managing them carefully to avoid conflicting with existing task formatting.

faq.question: What's a good color scheme for status tracking?
faq.answer: A standard Red, Yellow, Green scale is a common, easy-to-scan choice for risk or priority tracking.
